sector.name: Asset & Wealth Mgmt
sector.description: AMCs, wealth managers and digital investing platforms

companies: Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC Ltd
ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company Ltd
Nippon Life India Asset Management Ltd

fyPerformance: Total AUM stood at ₹6.7 lakh Cr; Total ARR AUM stood at ₹3,11,940 Cr (+26% YoY)
Wealth ARR AUM stood at ₹2,16,000 Cr and Asset Management ARR AUM stood at ₹95,000 Cr
FY26 ARR net flows stood at ₹55,875 Cr; organic ARR net flows stood at ₹35,199 Cr (+36% YoY)
FY26 ARR revenue stood at ₹2,289 Cr (+35% YoY), now forming 75% of operating revenue
FY26 PAT stood at ₹1,225 Cr (+21% YoY); Q4FY26 ARR revenue stood at ₹605 Cr (+20% YoY)
360 ONE WAM Ltd.outlook: FY26 was a landmark year despite volatile markets. Wealth and Asset Management remained resilient, supported by SIPs, IPO monetisation, block deals, PE exits and rising alternatives demand. B&K integration is complete, UBS collaboration is showing early traction, and investment banking is expected to contribute meaningfully over the next 12-18 months.
highlights: ARR retention stood at 78 bps, with Wealth at 76 bps and Asset Management at 83 bps
Wealth net flows stood at ₹25,900 Cr, while Asset Management net flows stood at ₹9,299 Cr
HNI Reserve program has around 60 RMs across 12 locations, managing nearly ₹4,000 Cr AUM for 650+ clients
ET Money business is expected to move towards breakeven in the near term
360 ONE Capital covers 500+ mid and small-cap companies and serves 300+ institutional clients
UBS collaboration can help alternatives and listed strategies access offshore capital
360 ONE WAM Ltd.quote: "360 ONE is outperforming the sector on ARR AUM growth, UHNI franchise strength, net flows and platform expansion."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹1,198 Cr (+22% YoY) and FY26 PAT stood at ₹386 Cr (+28% YoY)
Reported FY26 revenue stood at ₹1,253 Cr (+28% YoY) and reported PAT stood at ₹397 Cr (+32% YoY)
Q4FY26 adjusted revenue stood at ₹302 Cr (+25% YoY), while adjusted PAT stood at ₹92 Cr (+25% YoY)
FY26 mutual fund distribution revenue stood at ₹494 Cr (+22% YoY)
FY26 net flows stood at ₹13,457 Cr (+7% YoY)
highlights: AUM crossed ₹1 lakh Cr after March-end, supported by market recovery
Client families increased by 1,872 on net basis to 13,395
AUM lost due to client attrition remained very low at 0.54%
Digital wealth / B2B2C AUM stood at ₹2,218 Cr, up 22%
OFA SaaS platform crossed 6,900+ subscribers and platform assets stood at ₹1.47 lakh Cr
Management highlighted the 18th consecutive quarter of 20%+ YoY PAT growth
Anand Rathi Wealth Ltd.quote: "Anand Rathi is outperforming the sector on PAT consistency, client retention, AUM growth and market-agnostic wealth model."

fyPerformance: Total client assets stood at ₹4.5 lakh Cr and FY26 operating PAT crossed ₹1,000 Cr
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹825 Cr (+7% YoY); Q4FY26 operating PAT stood at ₹269 Cr (+5% YoY)
FY26 ROE stood at 28%
Wealth client assets stood at ₹1.1 lakh Cr (+14% YoY)
MPIS assets stood at ₹39,000 Cr and FY26 net new money stood at ₹8,900 Cr
Nuvama Wealth Management Ltd.outlook: FY26 was resilient despite market volatility and moderation in institutional equities and investment banking. FY27 will see incremental investments in Asset Management, new strategies, MF licence and SIF business, while recurring revenue mix continues to improve.
highlights: Excluding IE and IB, Q4FY26 revenue grew 13% and FY26 revenue grew 17%
Wealth and Private together grew 20% YoY
Wealth, Asset Management and Asset Services now contribute around 80% of firm revenue vs 74% last year
Wealth retention remained stable around 90 bps
Private client assets / ARR grew 22%, net new money grew 24% and revenue grew 24%
Asset Management management fee income grew 31%
Nuvama Wealth Management Ltd.quote: "Nuvama is outperforming the sector on wealth/private growth, recurring revenue mix, ROE and operating leverage."

fyPerformance: FY26 average daily AUM stood at ₹1.21 lakh Cr; closing AUM on 31 March stood at ₹1.19 lakh Cr
AUM on 5 May stood at ₹1.33 lakh Cr, around 10% higher than FY26 average AUM
Q4FY26 average AUM stood at ₹1,28,000 Cr (+26% YoY)
FY26 equity net sales stood at ₹13,900 Cr
MF revenue grew 21%, total revenue from operations grew 19%, operating profit grew 18% and PAT grew 14%
Prudent Corporate Advisory Services Ltd.outlook: AUM recovery after March gives a strong FY27 revenue tailwind. Management remains confident in SIP behaviour despite higher cancellations during weak markets, while insurance, SIF, AI-led partner tools and B2B2C distribution remain key growth levers.
highlights: Q4 saw highest-ever quarterly equity net sales of ₹4,300 Cr
New SIP registrations were the highest-ever on annual basis
Added around 5,100 new partners during the year
Indus acquisition integration progressed well, with 100% manpower retention
Prudent Edge AI platform launched for mutual fund distribution partners
Insurance business continues to scale, especially health insurance
Prudent Corporate Advisory Services Ltd.quote: "Prudent is outperforming the sector on B2B2C distribution, partner network, SIP book, equity net sales and technology-led scale."

fyPerformance: Consolidated PAT stood at ₹547 Cr (+27% YoY)
Alternative Asset Management FPAUM stood at ₹44,000 Cr (+32% YoY)
Mutual Fund equity AUM stood at ₹78,000 Cr (+25% YoY)
ARC recoveries stood at ₹8,590 Cr
MSME disbursements grew around 200%, Housing Finance disbursements grew 27% and General Insurance GWP grew 28%
Edelweiss Financial Services Ltd.outlook: Edelweiss is improving due to growth in alternatives, EAAA IPO optionality, ARC recovery and debt reduction triggers. Corporate debt reduction remains a key focus, with expected cash realisations from IPO, stake sales and dividends.
highlights: EAAA received SEBI approval for IPO and completed 4.4% placement, raising ₹375 Cr
Citius transportation InvIT listed with portfolio value around ₹11,000 Cr
Carlyle investment in Nido has received CCI approval, RBI approval awaited
ARC recoveries remain strong and management expects ARC opportunity to improve again
Private credit FPAUM is around ₹20,000 Cr, with nearly 65% in special situations
Special situations products target 18%+ rupee yield
Edelweiss Financial Services Ltd.quote: "Edelweiss is improving due to growth in alternatives, EAAA IPO optionality, ARC recovery and debt reduction triggers."

fyPerformance: Reported PAT after minority stood at ₹1,202 Cr (+46% YoY)
Adjusted operating PAT stood at ₹1,133 Cr (+38% YoY); ROE stood at 12%
CACM FY26 net revenue stood at ₹789 Cr (+11% YoY) and operating PAT stood at ₹347 Cr
Private Markets operating PAT after minority stood at ₹543 Cr, up 3.6x
Wealth FY26 net revenue stood at ₹775 Cr (+9% YoY) and operating PAT stood at ₹132 Cr
JM Financial Ltd.outlook: FY26 was the second year after JM Financial's strategic pivot. Management expects CACM execution to improve once markets become more receptive, and wants Private Markets to evolve towards a fee-based, syndication-led and liquidity-provider model.
highlights: Corporate Advisory and Capital Markets closed 41 capital market transactions worth around ₹95,000 Cr in FY26
IPO pipeline remains strong, with documents filed for 55 IPOs aggregating ₹1,40,000 Cr issue size
Private Markets loan book reduced from ₹10,000 Cr in FY24 to around ₹4,000 Cr in FY26
Private Markets loan book is expected to remain around ₹4,000-5,000 Cr and grow 15-20% YoY
Wealth sales and RM strength increased 30% YoY to 1,046 employees
Recurring AUM across wealth businesses grew 10% YoY to around ₹31,000 Cr
JM Financial Ltd.quote: "JM Financial is improving on strategic pivot, CACM pipeline, Private Markets profitability and wealth franchise expansion."

fyPerformance: Operating PAT stood at ₹2,360 Cr (+16% YoY), while Q4 operating PAT grew 25%
Q4 exit operating PAT run-rate stood at ₹661 Cr
Asset & Private Wealth profits grew 33%
Asset & Private Wealth now contribute around 50% of operating PAT
Asset & Private Wealth AUM stood at ₹3.7 lakh Cr (+34% YoY), with net flows of ₹70,000 Cr
Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd.outlook: FY26 growth was led by annuity businesses, mainly Asset Management and Private Wealth. Management expects annuity businesses, which now contribute over 60% of group revenue, to keep rising as a share of the business.
highlights: AMC AUM crossed ₹1.5 lakh Cr across active, passive, AIF and PMS
AMC average AUM moved from ₹1.57 lakh Cr to ₹1.8 lakh Cr in April after market recovery
AMC unique PAN crossed 1 Cr vs 77 lakh last year
AMC has around 16% client share of the MF industry
Wealth business serves around 9,000 families, with around 50% having UHNI potential
Long-term credit rating was upgraded to AA+ stable
Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd.quote: "Motilal Oswal is outperforming the sector on annuity business growth, Asset & Private Wealth scale, net flows and operating PAT resilience."

fyPerformance: Total AUM stood at ₹7.73 lakh Cr and MF QAAUM stood at ₹7.25 lakh Cr (+30% YoY)
Overall AUM market share stood at 8.89%
FY26 operating profit stood at ₹1,748 Cr (+24% YoY); FY26 PAT stood at ₹1,529 Cr (+19% YoY)
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹739 Cr (+30% YoY), operating profit stood at ₹493 Cr and PAT stood at ₹385 Cr (+29% YoY)
Dividend payout stood at 91.5% of net profit
Nippon Life India Asset Management Ltd.outlook: NAM India was the fastest-growing AMC among Top-10 AMCs in Q4FY26 and FY26, with the highest increase in AUM market share in the industry. SIP, ETF, investor base and digital transaction scale remain core advantages.
highlights: Market share of 8.89% is the highest since June 2019
Monthly systematic book stood at ₹3,720 Cr in March 2026, up 17%
SIP market share stood around 9.84%
NAM India remains the largest MF investor base with 23.8 mn unique investors
ETF AUM stood at ₹2.42 lakh Cr with ETF market share of 21.4%
Digital channel contributed 77% of new purchase transactions
Nippon Life India Asset Management Ltd.quote: "NAM India is outperforming the sector on market share gains, ETF leadership, investor base, SIP scale and digital transactions."

fyPerformance: Overall QAAUM stood at ₹9.3 lakh Cr (+20% YoY), with equity-oriented AUM at ₹6 lakh Cr
SIP + STP flows in March stood at ₹4,880 Cr (+33% YoY)
Total accounts crossed 30 mn and unique investors stood at 16.7 mn
FY26 total revenue stood at ₹4,620 Cr; revenue from operations stood at ₹4,120 Cr (+18% YoY)
Operating profit stood at ₹3,210 Cr (+18% YoY) and PAT stood at ₹2,860 Cr (+16% YoY)
HDFC Asset Management Company Ltd.outlook: HDFC AMC continues to benefit from financialisation, SIP growth, digital adoption and scale. Management's digital strategy is to become a digital AI wealth creator for every Indian, with AI embedded across marketing, client engagement, investments, risk and compliance.
highlights: Industry added 7.2 mn new MF investors during the year, while HDFC AMC alone added 3.5 mn unique investors
Direct plans now form around 31% of equity AUM
97% of transactions are digital vs 81% three years ago and 69% six years ago
HDFC AMC launched 7 new schemes during FY26
Alternatives business saw first close of private credit fund with IFC as anchor and partner
Operating margin stood at 35 bps of AUM and dividend payout ratio stood at 81%
HDFC Asset Management Company Ltd.quote: "HDFC AMC is outperforming the sector on scale, unique investor addition, digital adoption, operating profit and PAT delivery."

Unique customer base stood at 17 mn; March systematic transactions stood at ₹5,104 Cr (+31% YoY)
Q4 operating revenue stood at ₹1,517 Cr (+20% YoY); Q4 PAT stood at ₹763 Cr (+10% YoY)
ICICI Prudential Asset Management Compan.outlook: ICICI Prudential AMC remains one of the largest AMCs in India and leads active, equity-oriented and equity-oriented hybrid categories. SIF, alternatives, GIFT City and Dubai presence are being scaled for domestic and offshore opportunities.
highlights: Total MF market share stood around 13.5%
Active schemes market share stood around 13.7%, where company is the largest
Equity and equity-oriented market share stood around 14.2%, where company is the largest
Equity-oriented hybrid market share stood around 26.7%, where company is the largest
Passive AUM grew strongly, up 48% YoY
FY26 gross yield stood at 52 bps, net yield at 48 bps and operating margin improved to 38 bps
ICICI Prudential Asset Management Compan.quote: "ICICI Prudential AMC is outperforming the sector on active AUM scale, hybrid leadership, operating margins, ROE and SIF / alternatives build-out."

Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC Ltd.outlook: ABSL AMC is improving with passive growth, SIP pickup, alternatives scale-up and SIF launch. The company is building GIFT City, passive, PMS/AIF, real estate and SIF platforms alongside the core MF franchise.
highlights: Q4 new SIP registrations stood around 6 lakh, up 16% QoQ
Company is present across 19,000+ pin codes
PMS + AIF assets grew to ₹32,570 Cr, nearly 3x YoY
Passive QAAUM crossed ₹41,200 Cr, up 25%, while ETF QAAUM grew 68% YoY

SIF vertical was launched under APEX SIF brand
Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC Ltd.quote: "ABSL AMC is improving with strong passive growth, SIP pickup, alternatives scale-up and SIF launch."

fyPerformance: Q4 orders stood at 431 mn, a 6-quarter high
Average daily orders stood at 7.4 mn in March 2026
Retail equity turnover share stood at 20.4% and demat market share stood at 16.7%
Q4 gross income stood at ₹1,470 Cr (+10% QoQ), while Q4 net income stood at ₹1,130 Cr (+10% QoQ)
Q4 PAT stood at ₹320 Cr (+19% QoQ), with EBIDAT margin of 42%
Angel One Ltd.outlook: Angel One is moving from pure broking towards a broader financial services platform. FY27 focus remains on wealth expansion, NBFC and distribution platforms, AMC products and AI-led productivity.
highlights: Average daily orders recovered strongly from 5 mn in February 2025 to 7.4 mn in March 2026
Ionic Wealth AUM crossed ₹10,000 Cr, up 23% QoQ
UHNI wealth business doubled over the last 12 months and wealth client base crossed 1,900+
More than 45% of wealth clients are from non-metros
AMC now has 11 schemes and folios grew 28% QoQ to 2.46 lakh+
More than 50% of development work is AI-augmented and 80% of Ionic Wealth codebase is AI-generated
Angel One Ltd.quote: "Angel One is outperforming the sector on order recovery, market share, digital scale, wealth expansion and AI-led productivity."

fyPerformance: Management did not disclose detailed FY26 revenue or PAT numbers in the transcript
FY26 was an important year as Groww became a public company
AMC AUM grew around 2.5x in one year
Company scaled MTF meaningfully
Market share improved across equity, MF and F&O
Billionbrains Garage Ventures Ltd.outlook: Groww continues to see a long runway for financialisation in India. FY27 focus will remain on scaling wealth, growing market share, launching new products and using AI for customer experience and internal productivity.
highlights: FY26 saw regulatory changes in broking like True to Label and F&O changes, but company managed the transition well
Company launched new products like commodities and bonds
Wealth management foray was made through Fisdom acquisition
Groww launched W for affluent / HNI customers and Groww Prime for mass affluent customers
Customer acquisition funnel shifted more towards MF and ETFs due to weak markets
Management said most customer cohorts remain profitable after market recovery post March correction
Billionbrains Garage Ventures Ltd.quote: "Groww is outperforming on digital scale, customer acquisition, market share gains and multi-product expansion, but detailed profitability needs to be tracked once disclosed."
